title: iris pseudopumila subsp. pseudopumila
intro: Iris pseudopumila subsp. pseudopumila, also known as the dwarf iris or false sea-daffodil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the iris family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Iris pseudopumila subsp. pseudopumila, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
